ranked_movies.title: Children of Men
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively praised in its own dedicated thread for its powerful dystopian vision, incredible one-shot takes, emotional depth, and increasing relevance. Users called it a 'perfect movie' and one of the best dystopian films ever made.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in two separate threads seeking traumatizing and mind-blowing documentaries. It is consistently cited as a profoundly unsettling and eye-opening film that changes one's perspective.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a prime example of a film that is both artistically made and deeply traumatizing. The discussion around its use of sound to induce physical reactions in the audience highlights its intentional and powerful impact.

ranked_movies.title: One Battle After Another
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of multiple in-depth analyses and discussions on r/TrueFilm. It is dissected for its political commentary, technical prowess in action sequences, and exploration of revolution and activism, marking it as a significant new film.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: The original catalyst for the top post seeking 'must watch' traumatizing movies. It is the benchmark against which other disturbing films are measured, establishing its notoriety and impact.

ranked_movies.title: Free Solo
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times in threads seeking jaw-dropping and mind-blowing documentaries. Its portrayal of Alex Honnold's free solo climb is consistently cited as an intense and breathtaking experience.

ranked_movies.title: Lost in Translation
ranked_movies.reason: Top recommendation in a thread seeking movies about 'people being real.' It is celebrated for its beautiful, quiet human moments, awkwardness, and growth, perfectly fitting the request.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: A highly recommended documentary miniseries that was described as mind-blowing and full of unbelievable moments, particularly regarding the story of Robert Durst.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a chilling 'comedy' that will severely fuck you up. It is part of a list of films known for their dark themes and traumatic impact.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as a film that is extremely dark and shakes viewers to their core. It is highlighted for its handling of heavy themes like child SA.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a jaw-dropping documentary that presents an unbelievable true story, making viewers question their perception of reality.

ranked_movies.title: The Station Agent
ranked_movies.reason: Top recommendation for a film about 'people being real.' It is praised for its quiet, human moments and early Peter Dinklage performance.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a traumatizing and fucked-up movie with an ending that is 'burned in the memory' of those who see it. It is noted for its gritty, realistic portrayal of teenagers.

ranked_movies.title: Watership Down
ranked_movies.reason: Explicitly recommended for its ability to 'fuck you the fuck up.' Its reputation as a deeply disturbing animated film secures its place on this list.

ranked_movies.title: Pirates of the Caribbean
ranked_movies.reason: The top answer to a post about movies that exceeded expectations. It was a surprise blockbuster hit based on a Disney ride that became a massive cultural phenomenon.

ranked_movies.title: A Clockwork Orange
ranked_movies.reason: Part of a list of films that gave viewers nightmares for weeks. It is a classic example of a disturbing yet artistically acclaimed movie.

ranked_movies.title: Trainspotting
ranked_movies.reason: Included in a list of movies known for their traumatic and lasting impact on viewers, fitting the request for films that are more than just suffering porn.

ranked_movies.title: Man on Wire
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a jaw-dropping documentary about Philippe Petit's illegal tightrope walk between the WTC towers, an amazing and unbelievable true story.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a movie that far exceeded expectations. It is praised for being very funny and having a perfect cast, becoming a beloved cult classic.

ranked_movies.title: Shaun of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that was much better than expected, winning over skeptical viewers and becoming a classic of its genre.
